records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Rights of the Animal Upon Its Owner
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2468 - The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings of Allah (swt) be upon him and his family) said:
"If a mount stumbles under its rider and the rider says to it, 'May you perish!' it responds, 'Perished is the one who disobeys our Lord (azj) the most.'"
records.arabicText: 2468 - وَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : «إِذَا عَثَرَتِ اَلدَّابَّةُ تَحْتَ اَلرَّجُلِ فَقَالَ لَهَا تَعَسْتِ تَقُولُ تَعَسَ أَعْصَانَا لِلرَّبِّ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2468 - The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings of Allah (swt) be upon him and his family) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If a mount stumbles under its rider and the rider says to it, 'May you perish!' it responds, 'Perished is the one who disobeys our Lord (azj) the most.'"

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Rights of the Animal Upon Its Owner
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2469 - Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said:
"Regarding animals: Do not strike their faces and do not curse them, for Allah (swt), the Mighty and Glorious, curses the one who curses them."
In another narration, Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said:
"Do not insult their faces."
records.arabicText: 2469 - وَ قَالَ عَلِيٌّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «فِي اَلدَّوَابِّ لاَ تَضْرِبُوا اَلْوُجُوهَ وَ لاَ تَلْعَنُوهَا فَإِنَّ اَللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ لَعَنَ لاَعِنَهَا » وَ فِي خَبَرٍ آخَرَ «لاَ تُقَبِّحُوا اَلْوُجُوهَ.

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Rights of the Animal Upon Its Owner
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2470 - The Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah (swt) be upon him and his family) said:
"When animals are cursed, the curse clings to them."
records.arabicText: 2470 - وَ قَالَ اَلنَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : «إِنَّ اَلدَّوَابَّ إِذَا لُعِنَتْ لَزِمَتْهَا اَللَّعْنَةُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2470 - The Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah (swt) be upon him and his family) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "When animals are cursed, the curse clings to them."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Rights of the Animal Upon Its Owner
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2471 - The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings of Allah (swt) be upon him and his family) said:
"Do not sit sideways on animals, and do not make their backs your seats."
records.arabicText: 2471 - وَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : «لاَ تَتَوَرَّكُوا عَلَى اَلدَّوَابِّ وَ لاَ تَتَّخِذُوا ظُهُورَهَا مَجَالِسَ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2471 - The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings of Allah (swt) be upon him and his family) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Do not sit sideways on animals, and do not make their backs your seats."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Rights of the Animal Upon Its Owner
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2472 - Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as)  said:
"Everything has a sanctity, and the sanctity of animals lies in their faces."
records.arabicText: 2472 - وَ قَالَ اَلْبَاقِرُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «لِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ حُرْمَةٌ وَ حُرْمَةُ اَلْبَهَائِمِ فِي وُجُوهِهَا ».

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What Has Not Been Made Obscure Regarding Livestock
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2473 - Ali ibn Ri'ab narrated from Abu Hamzah, from Imam Ali ibn al-Husayn (as), that he used to say:
"Animals are not ignorant of four things: their knowledge of their Lord (azj), the Blessed and Exalted; their knowledge of death; their distinction between male and female; and their knowledge of fertile pasture."
records.arabicText: 2473 - رَوَى عَلِيُّ بْنُ رِئَابٍ عَنْ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ اَلْحُسَيْنِ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَقُولُ : «مَا بُهِمَتِ اَلْبَهَائِمُ عَنْهُ فَلَمْ تُبْهَمْ عَنْ أَرْبَعَةٍ مَعْرِفَتِهَا بِالرَّبِّ تَبَارَكَ وَ تَعَالَى وَ مَعْرِفَتِهَا بِالْمَوْتِ وَ مَعْرِفَتِهَا بِالْأُنْثَى مِنَ اَلذَّكَرِ وَ مَعْرِفَتِهَا بِالْمَرْعَى اَلْخِصْبِ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2473 - Ali ibn Ri'ab narrated from Abu Hamzah, from Imam Ali ibn al-Husayn (as), that he used to say:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Animals are not ignorant of four things: their knowledge of their Lord (azj), the Blessed and Exalted; their knowledge of death; their distinction between male and female; and their knowledge of fertile pasture."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What Has Not Been Made Obscure Regarding Livestock
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2474 - As for the narration from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) in which he said:
"If animals knew about death what you know, you would never eat a fat one among them."
records.arabicText: 2474 - وَ أَمَّا اَلْخَبَرُ اَلَّذِي رُوِيَ عَنِ اَلصَّادِقِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : «لَوْ عَرَفَتِ اَلْبَهَائِمُ مِنَ اَلْمَوْتِ مَا تَعْرِفُونَ مَا أَكَلْتُمْ مِنْهَا سَمِيناً قَطُّ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2474 - As for the narration from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) in which he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If animals knew about death what you know, you would never eat a fat one among them."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Reward for Spending on Horses
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2475 - The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings of Allah (swt) be upon him and his family) said regarding the verse of Allah (swt), the Mighty and Glorious:
"Those who spend their wealth in the night and the day, secretly and openly, they shall have their reward with their Lord (azj), and they shall not fear nor grieve." (Surah Al-Baqarah 2:274)
Prophet (sw) said: "It was revealed concerning spending on horses."
records.arabicText:
2475 - قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : فِي قَوْلِ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ «۝ اَلَّذِينَ يُنْفِقُونَ أَمْوٰالَهُمْ بِاللَّيْلِ وَ اَلنَّهٰارِ سِرًّا وَ عَلاٰنِيَةً فَلَهُمْ أَجْرُهُمْ عِنْدَ رَبِّهِمْ وَ لاٰ خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَ لاٰ هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ ۝»
قَالَ «نَزَلَتْ فِي اَلنَّفَقَةِ عَلَى اَلْخَيْلِ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2475 - The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings of Allah (swt) be upon him and his family) said regarding the verse of Allah (swt), the Mighty and Glorious:
"Those who spend their wealth in the night and the day, secretly and openly, they shall have their reward with their Lord (azj), and they shall not fear nor grieve." (Surah Al-Baqarah 2:274)
Prophet (sw) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "It was revealed concerning spending on horses."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Reason for the Two Patches on the Palms of the animal's Feet
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2476 - Hammad ibn Uthman narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
I said to him, "May I be your ransom! We see markings on the undersides of the forelegs of animals, like two patches or brands. What is that?"
Imam (as) replied: "That is the place where their nostrils were positioned while they were in their mother's womb."
records.arabicText: 2476 - رَوَى حَمَّادُ بْنُ عُثْمَانَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لَهُ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ نَرَى اَلدَّوَابَّ فِي بُطُونِ أَيْدِيهَا مِثْلُ اَلرُّقْعَتَيْنِ فِي بَاطِنِ يَدَيْهَا مِثْلُ اَلْكَيِّ فَأَيُّ شَيْءٍ هُوَ قَالَ «ذَلِكَ مَوْضِعُ مَنْخِرَيْهِ فِي بَطْنِ أُمِّهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2476 - Hammad ibn Uthman narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I said to him, "May I be your ransom! We see markings on the undersides of the forelegs of animals, like two patches or brands. What is that?"
Imam (as) replied: "That is the place where their nostrils were positioned while they were in their mother's womb."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Good Care for Animals
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2477 - It is narrated from Abu Dharr (may Allah (swt) have mercy on him) that he said:
I heard the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace of Allah (swt) be upon him and his family) say:
"The animal prays: 'O Allah (swt), grant me an owner of sincerity who feeds me, gives me drink, and does not burden me with more than I can bear.'"
records.arabicText: 2477 - رُوِيَ عَنْ أَبِي ذَرٍّ رَحْمَةُ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ أَنَّهُ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ يَقُولُ : «إِنَّ اَلدَّابَّةَ تَقُولُ: اَللَّهُمَّ اُرْزُقْنِي مَلِيكَ صِدْقٍ يُشْبِعُنِي وَ يَسْقِينِي وَ لاَ يُحَمِّلُنِي مَا لاَ أُطِيقُ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2477 - It is narrated from Abu Dharr (may Allah (swt) have mercy on him) that he said:
I heard the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace of Allah (swt) be upon him and his family) say:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The animal prays: 'O Allah (swt), grant me an owner of sincerity who feeds me, gives me drink, and does not burden me with more than I can bear.'"

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Good Care for Animals
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2478 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"No one purchases an animal except that it says: 'O Allah (swt), make him merciful to me.'"
records.arabicText: 2478 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «مَا اِشْتَرَى أَحَدٌ دَابَّةً إِلاَّ قَالَتْ: اَللَّهُمَّ اِجْعَلْهُ بِي رَحِيماً ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2478 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "No one purchases an animal except that it says: 'O Allah (swt), make him merciful to me.'"

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Good Care for Animals
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2479 - It is narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan, who said that he heard from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as):
"Acquire a mount, for it is an adornment, needs are fulfilled through it, and its sustenance is upon Allah (swt), the Mighty and Glorious."
records.arabicText: 2479 - وَ رَوَى عَنْهُ عَبْدُ اَللَّهِ بْنُ سِنَانٍ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : «اِتَّخِذُوا اَلدَّابَّةَ فَإِنَّهَا زَيْنٌ وَ تُقْضَى عَلَيْهَا اَلْحَوَائِجُ وَ رِزْقُهَا عَلَى اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2479 - It is narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan, who said that he heard from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Acquire a mount, for it is an adornment, needs are fulfilled through it, and its sustenance is upon Allah (swt), the Mighty and Glorious."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Good Care for Animals
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2480 - Al-Sakuni narrated with his chain of transmission that the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings of Allah (swt) be upon him and his family) said:
"Indeed, Allah (swt), the Blessed and Exalted, loves gentleness and facilitates it. When you ride lean animals, grant them proper rest. If the land is barren, travel quickly on them. If the land is fertile, let them rest in appropriate places."
records.arabicText: 2480 - وَ رَوَى اَلسَّكُونِيُّ بِإِسْنَادِهِ قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : «إِنَّ اَللَّهَ تَبَارَكَ وَ تَعَالَى يُحِبُّ اَلرِّفْقَ وَ يُعِينُ عَلَيْهِ فَإِذَا رَكِبْتُمُ اَلدَّوَابَّ اَلْعِجَافَ فَأَنْزِلُوهَا مَنَازِلَهَا فَإِنْ كَانَتِ اَلْأَرْضُ مُجْدِبَةً فَانْجُوا عَلَيْهَا وَ إِنْ كَانَتْ مُخْصِبَةً فَأَنْزِلُوهَا مَنَازِلَهَا».

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Good Care for Animals
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2481 - Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said:
"Whoever among you travels with an animal should begin, when stopping, by feeding and giving water to it."
records.arabicText: 2481 - وَ قَالَ عَلِيٌّ صَلَوَاتُ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ : «مَنْ سَافَرَ مِنْكُمْ بِدَابَّةٍ فَلْيَبْدَأْ حِينَ يَنْزِلُ بِعَلْفِهَا وَ سَقْيِهَا».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2481 - Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever among you travels with an animal should begin, when stopping, by feeding and giving water to it."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Good Care for Animals
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2482 - Abu Ja‘far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
"If you travel through fertile land, proceed gently, and if you travel through barren land, hasten your journey."
records.arabicText: 2482 - وَ قَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «إِذَا سِرْتَ فِي أَرْضٍ خِصْبَةٍ فَارْفُقْ بِالسَّيْرِ وَ إِذَا سِرْتَ فِي أَرْضٍ مُجْدِبَةٍ فَعَجِّلْ بِالسَّيْرِ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2482 - Abu Ja‘far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If you travel through fertile land, proceed gently, and if you travel through barren land, hasten your journey."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What Has Been Narrated Regarding Camels
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2483 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"Beware of red camels, for they have the shortest lifespan among camels."
records.arabicText: 2483 - قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «إِيَّاكُمْ وَ اَلْإِبِلَ اَلْحُمْرَ فَإِنَّهَا أَقْصَرُ اَلْإِبِلِ أَعْمَاراً».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2483 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Beware of red camels, for they have the shortest lifespan among camels."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What Has Been Narrated Regarding Camels
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2484 - Imam (as) said:
"On the top of every camel sits a devil, so feed it well and keep it under control."
records.arabicText: 2484 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «إِنَّ عَلَى ذِرْوَةِ كُلِّ بَعِيرٍ شَيْطَاناً فَأَشْبِعْهُ وَ اِمْتَهِنْهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2484 - Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "On the top of every camel sits a devil, so feed it well and keep it under control."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What Has Been Narrated Regarding Camels
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2485 - Abu Abdullah (as) said:
"Purchase black, unattractive camels, for they have the longest lifespans among camels."
records.arabicText: 2485 - وَ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «اِشْتَرُوا اَلسُّودَ اَلْقِبَاحَ فَإِنَّهَا أَطْوَلُ اَلْإِبِلِ أَعْمَاراً ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2485 - Abu Abdullah (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Purchase black, unattractive camels, for they have the longest lifespans among camels."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What Has Been Narrated Regarding Camels
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2486 - The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings of Allah (swt) be upon him and his family) said:
"Camels are a source of honor for their owners."
records.arabicText: 2486 - وَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : «اَلْإِبِلُ عِزٌّ لِأَهْلِهَا ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2486 - The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings of Allah (swt) be upon him and his family) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Camels are a source of honor for their owners."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What Has Been Narrated Regarding Camels
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2487 - The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings of Allah (swt) be upon him and his family) forbade stepping over the camel line (caravan).
It was asked: "O Messenger of Allah (swt), why?"
Prophet (sw) replied: "Because there is not a single camel line except that between one camel and the next there is a devil."
records.arabicText: 2487 - وَ نَهَى رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : أَنْ يُتَخَطَّى اَلْقِطَارُ قِيلَ يَا رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ وَ لِمَ قَالَ «لِأَنَّهُ لَيْسَ مِنْ قِطَارٍ إِلاَّ وَ مَا بَيْنَ اَلْبَعِيرِ إِلَى اَلْبَعِيرِ شَيْطَانٌ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2487 -
records.thaqalaynMatn:
The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings of Allah (swt) be upon him and his family) forbade stepping over the camel line (caravan).
It was asked: "O Messenger of Allah (swt), why?"
Prophet (sw) replied: "Because there is not a single camel line except that between one camel and the next there is a devil."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What Has Been Narrated Regarding Camels
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2488 - The Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah (swt) be upon him and his family) was asked: "Which wealth is best?"
Prophet (sw) replied: "A crop that its owner sows, tends to, and gives its due when it is harvested."
He was asked: "O Messenger of Allah (swt), which wealth is best after crops?"
Prophet (sw) replied: "A man with his sheep, following rain-fed pastures, who establishes prayer and pays zakat."
He was asked: "O Messenger of Allah (swt), which wealth is best after sheep?"
Prophet (sw) replied: "Cattle, which bring benefit in the morning and in the evening."
He was asked: "O Messenger of Allah (swt), which wealth is best after cattle?"
Prophet (sw) replied: "Date palms, which are steadfast in mud and provide food during drought. Indeed, palm trees are excellent. However, whoever sells them finds their price like ashes blown by a stormy wind atop a high mountain, unless he plants their replacement."
He was asked: "O Messenger of Allah (swt), which wealth is best after date palms?"
The Prophet remained silent.
A man then asked: "What about camels, O Messenger of Allah (swt)?"
Prophet (sw) replied: "In them is hardship, roughness, and toil. They are far removed (from human company), leaving in the morning and returning in the evening. Their benefit comes only from their left side. Indeed, they are not free from the wicked and the disobedient."
records.arabicText: 2488 - وَ سُئِلَ اَلنَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : أَيُّ اَلْمَالِ خَيْرٌ، قَالَ «زَرْعٌ زَرَعَهُ صَاحِبُهُ وَ أَصْلَحَهُ وَ أَدَّى حَقَّهُ يَوْمَ حَصَادِهِ» قِيلَ يَا رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ فَأَيُّ اَلْمَالِ بَعْدَ اَلزَّرْعِ خَيْرٌ قَالَ «رَجُلٌ فِي غَنَمِهِ قَدْ تَبِعَ بِهَا مَوَاضِعَ اَلْقَطْرِ يُقِيمُ اَلصَّلاَةَ وَ يُؤْتِي اَلزَّكَاةَ» قِيلَ يَا رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ فَأَيُّ اَلْمَالِ بَعْدَ اَلْغَنَمِ خَيْرٌ قَالَ «اَلْبَقَرُ تَغْدُو بِخَيْرٍ وَ تَرُوحُ بِخَيْرٍ» قِيلَ يَا رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ فَأَيُّ اَلْمَالِ بَعْدَ اَلْبَقَرِ خَيْرٌ فَقَالَ «اَلرَّاسِيَاتُ فِي اَلْوَحَلِ اَلْمُطْعِمَاتُ فِي اَلْمَحْلِ ، نِعْمَ اَلشَّيْءُ اَلنَّخْلُ مَنْ بَاعَهُ فَإِنَّمَا ثَمَنُهُ بِمَنْزِلَةِ رَمَادٍ عَلَى رَأْسِ شَاهِقَةٍ «اِشْتَدَّتْ بِهِ اَلرِّيحُ فِي يَوْمٍ عٰاصِفٍ» إِلاَّ أَنْ يُخْلِفَ مَكَانَهَا» قِيلَ يَا رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ فَأَيُّ اَلْمَالِ بَعْدَ اَلنَّخْلِ خَيْرٌ فَسَكَتَ فَقَالَ لَهُ رَجُلٌ فَأَيْنَ اَلْإِبِلُ قَالَ «فِيهَا اَلشَّقَاءُ وَ اَلْجَفَاءُ وَ اَلْعَنَاءُ وَ بُعْدُ اَلدَّارِ تَغْدُو مُدْبِرَةً وَ تَرُوحُ مُدْبِرَةً لاَ يَأْتِي خَيْرُهَا إِلاَّ مِنْ جَانِبِهَا اَلْأَشْأَمِ أَمَا إِنَّهَا لاَ تَعْدَمُ اَلْأَشْقِيَاءَ اَلْفَجَرَةَ ».

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What Has Been Narrated Regarding Camels
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2489 - Imam (as) said:
"Regarding sheep: when they come forward, they bring blessings, and when they go away, they still bring blessings.
Regarding cattle: when they come forward, they bring blessings, and when they go away, they take blessings with them.
Regarding camels: when they come forward, they take blessings away, and when they go away, they take even more blessings away."
records.arabicText: 2489 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «فِي اَلْغَنَمِ إِذَا أَقْبَلَتْ أَقْبَلَتْ وَ إِذَا أَدْبَرَتْ أَقْبَلَتْ وَ اَلْبَقَرُ إِذَا أَقْبَلَتْ أَقْبَلَتْ وَ إِذَا أَدْبَرَتْ أَدْبَرَتْ وَ اَلْإِبِلُ إِذَا أَقْبَلَتْ أَدْبَرَتْ وَ إِذَا أَدْبَرَتْ أَدْبَرَتْ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2489 - Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Regarding sheep: when they come forward, they bring blessings, and when they go away, they still bring blessings.
Regarding cattle: when they come forward, they bring blessings, and when they go away, they take blessings with them.
Regarding camels: when they come forward, they take blessings away, and when they go away, they take even more blessings away."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What Justice is Obligatory Towards the Camel, Avoiding Beating it, and Refraining From Oppressing it
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2490 - Al-Sakuni narrated with his chain of transmission that the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah (swt) be upon him and his family) saw a camel tied up with its gear still on it.
Prophet (sw) said: "Where is its owner? Tell him to prepare for a dispute tomorrow (on the Day of Judgment)."
records.arabicText: 2490 - رَوَى اَلسَّكُونِيُّ بِإِسْنَادِهِ : أَنَّ اَلنَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ أَبْصَرَ نَاقَةً مَعْقُولَةً وَ عَلَيْهَا جَهَازُهَا فَقَالَ «أَيْنَ صَاحِبُهَا مُرُوهُ فَلْيَسْتَعِدَّ غَداً لِلْخُصُومَةِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2490 - Al-Sakuni narrated with his chain of transmission that the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah (swt) be upon him and his family) saw a camel tied up with its gear still on it.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Prophet (sw) said: "Where is its owner? Tell him to prepare for a dispute tomorrow (on the Day of Judgment)."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What Justice is Obligatory Towards the Camel, Avoiding Beating it, and Refraining From Oppressing it
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2491 - In another narration, the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah (swt) be upon him and his family) said:
"Delay the loading (of animals), for their front legs are suspended, and their hind legs are bound."
records.arabicText: 2491 - وَ فِي خَبَرٍ آخَرَ قَالَ اَلنَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : «أَخِّرُوا اَلْأَحْمَالَ فَإِنَّ اَلْيَدَيْنِ مُعَلَّقَةٌ وَ اَلرِّجْلَيْنِ مُوثَقَةٌ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2491 - In another narration, the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah (swt) be upon him and his family) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Delay the loading (of animals), for their front legs are suspended, and their hind legs are bound."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What Justice is Obligatory Towards the Camel, Avoiding Beating it, and Refraining From Oppressing it
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2492 - Ibn Faddal narrated from Hammad al-Lahham, who said:
A caravan passed by Abu Abdullah (as), and he noticed a pack camel that had leaned to one side under its load.
Imam (as) said: "O boy, adjust the load on this animal, for Allah (swt), the Exalted, loves justice."
records.arabicText: 2492 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ فَضَّالٍ عَنْ حَمَّادٍ اَللَّحَّامِ قَالَ : مَرَّ قِطَارٌ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فَرَأَى زَامِلَةً قَدْ مَالَتْ فَقَالَ «يَا غُلاَمُ اِعْدِلْ عَلَى هَذَا اَلْحَمْلِ فَإِنَّ اَللَّهَ تَعَالَى يُحِبُّ اَلْعَدْلَ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2492 - Ibn Faddal narrated from Hammad al-Lahham, who said:
A caravan passed by Abu Abdullah (as), and he noticed a pack camel that had leaned to one side under its load.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "O boy, adjust the load on this animal, for Allah (swt), the Exalted, loves justice."
